2606.07114.title: DIFFRACT: Neuralized Utility Maximization for Wireless Networks by Differentiable Programming
2606.07114.paper_summary: Next-generation wireless networks, including satellite-to-Open RAN systems, demand agile and intelligent resource management capable of handling dynamic multi-user interference under stochastic quality of service constraints. This paper introduces DIFFRACT, a neuralized utility maximization framework that leverages differentiable programming to integrate deep learning with optimization in wireless networks. Central to our approach is the exploitation of the mathematical structure of standard interference functions, which are foundational in wireless power control. By developing a duality theory for these functions, we map iterative interference management algorithms into differentiable neural network architectures via algorithm unrolling. This enables distributed, end-to-end gradient-based learning at the network edge, supporting real-time adaptation to interference in both terrestrial and non-terrestrial environments. DIFFRACT allows for scalable and robust utility maximization by modeling complex channel dynamics and leveraging the expressiveness of differentiable models. Experimental results confirm the framework's theoretical soundness and practical effectiveness for next-generation wireless systems.
2606.07114.paper_summary_zh: 下一代無線網絡，包括衛星到開放無線接入網（Open RAN）系統，要求具備靈活和智能的資源管理，能夠在隨機服務質量約束下處理動態的多用戶干擾。本文介紹了DIFFRACT，一個神經化的效用最大化框架，利用可微編程將深度學習與無線網絡中的優化相結合。我們方法的核心是利用標準干擾函數的數學結構，這些函數在無線功率控制中具有基礎性。通過為這些函數發展對偶理論，我們將迭代干擾管理算法映射到可微神經網絡架構中，這是通過算法展開實現的。這使得在網絡邊緣進行分散的端到端基於梯度的學習成為可能，支持在地面和非地面環境中對干擾的實時適應。DIFFRACT通過建模複雜的信道動態並利用可微模型的表達能力，實現可擴展和穩健的效用最大化。實驗結果證實了該框架的理論合理性和對於下一代無線系統的實際有效性。
2606.07114.author: Chee Wei Tan et.al.
